title: Sales Analysis Brief
description: Clean a retail dataset, answer three business questions and write a concise recommendation.
starterCode:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO

# Load the Retail Orders dataset here
# Then clean, analyse and summarise it.

rubric.description: Calculations and conclusions are supported by the data.
Duplicates, missing values and assumptions are handled clearly.
Code is readable, structured and reproducible.
Findings are concise and understandable to a stakeholder.
At least one meaningful limitation is documented.

title: API Response Automation
description: Process simulated API responses, handle failures and produce a clean company report.
starterCode:
import json
import pandas as pd

responses = [
    '{"status": 200, "company": "BioNova", "employees": 420}',
    '{"status": 503, "error": "Service unavailable"}',
    '{"status": 200, "company": "EduSpark"}'
]

# Build a reliable processing workflow below

rubric.description: Successful and failed responses are handled correctly.
Missing fields and invalid payloads do not break the workflow.
Important behaviour is protected by assertions or tests.
The solution uses clear functions and names.
The design choices are explained.

title: Validated ETL Pipeline
description: Build a small ETL pipeline with validation, logging, incremental processing and tests.
deliverables: Separate extract, transform and load stages
Logging of row counts and failures
starterCode:
import logging
import pandas as pd

logger = logging.getLogger('etl-assignment')

# Define extract, validate, transform and load functions below

rubric.description: ETL stages are separated and reusable.
Bad records are identified and handled transparently.
Logging, errors and checkpoints are implemented safely.
Core behaviour and edge cases are tested.
The README explains usage, assumptions and limitations.
